name: Austin, Texas
label: Childhood / Hometown
summary: Geeta Persad grew up in Austin, exploring the Texas Memorial Museum and attending lectures at UT Austin as a child. She returned here as faculty in 2020.

name: Stanford, California
label: Stanford University — B.S. Geophysics
summary: Persad earned her B.S. in Geophysics at Stanford University (2010), leaving Austin for the first time. She later returned to Stanford as a postdoctoral researcher at the Carnegie Institution for Science.

name: Princeton, New Jersey
label: Princeton University — Ph.D. Atmospheric & Oceanic Sciences
summary: Persad earned her Ph.D. in Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences at Princeton (2016), with an NSF Graduate Research Fellowship and HMEI-STEP policy certificate. Her dissertation focused on atmospheric aerosols and their climate impacts.

name: Stanford / Carnegie Institution
label: Carnegie Institution for Science & Union of Concerned Scientists
summary: After her PhD, Persad held a joint position as Research Associate at the Carnegie Institution for Science (Stanford) and Senior Climate Scientist at the Union of Concerned Scientists, applying climate modeling to Western U.S. water policy.

name: Austin, Texas
label: UT Austin — Jackson School of Geosciences
summary: Persad joined UT Austin's Jackson School of Geosciences as Assistant Professor of Climate Science in 2020 — returning to her hometown. She leads the Persad Aero-Climate Group and received the NSF CAREER Award in 2025.

name: Global climate research network
label: International conferences and collaborations
summary: Persad's research spans global aerosol transport, monsoon systems, and international climate policy — with presentations at NOAA, AGU, and international venues.
